sourceMovie.title: Pitch Perfect
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Pitch Perfect for the same reason it broke through in 2012: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a music rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Jason Moore keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and music blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Pitch Perfect 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Pitch Perfect clicked for you, Pitch Perfect 2 (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and music, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Pitch Perfect?
faq.answer: Try Pitch Perfect 2, Pitch Perfect 3, Wayne's World—each shares DNA with Pitch Perfect while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Pitch Perfect?
faq.answer: Pitch Perfect (2012) is best described as Comedy, Music, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Pitch Perfect part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Pitch Perfect Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
